title: Eight Misbehavin

jokes.text: These Swedish furniture designers sure have some far-out ideas. I mean, a green table. I wouldn't have thought of that in my wildest dreams.
jokes.setup: Homer admiring Swedish furniture design innovation
jokes.punchline: His amazement at the revolutionary concept of a green table
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his genuine amazement at something mundane reveals his simple worldview. Well-structured with believable setup leading to absurd conclusion.

jokes.text: Arr! She swallowed me whole. You put it together yourself. All you need is me-Allen Wrench.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain found inside furniture, speaking in pirate dialect
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Allen wrench' tool and his own name/identity
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works both as furniture instruction and character introduction. Economical and character-specific to Sea Captain's speech patterns.

jokes.text: He's named after what he is.
jokes.setup: Following the Allen wrench pun
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact observation about the obvious naming convention
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Homer moment. His literal interpretation and deadpan delivery work well as followup to the pun.

jokes.text: It's not a costume. They found me inside a meteor. Help. I need tungsten to live. Tungsten!
jokes.setup: Someone assumes it's a costume
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd backstory ending in desperate tungsten plea
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons random absurdity. The escalation from 'not a costume' to desperate alien needs is perfectly executed with good quotable elements.

jokes.text: You don't want something that overshadows the pencils.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting an elaborate pencil holder
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern about upstaging the pencils themselves
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her practical, overthinking nature applied to something trivial. Shows character through mundane situation.

jokes.text: Mom, no. Everyone at school picks on the Pöpli kids. Even I do. I just hate them so much.
jokes.setup: Bart objecting to a furniture choice
jokes.punchline: His honest admission of participating in and enjoying bullying
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart characterization. The escalation from practical concern to gleeful admission of cruelty is perfectly structured and character-specific. Dark but honest about kid behavior.

jokes.text: Yes, I will have the plopenlugen. And don't skimp on the doodlemunch.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering food at IKEA
jokes.punchline: Made-up Swedish-sounding food names delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Solid nonsense word comedy. Homer's confidence in ordering gibberish feels authentic to his character and the IKEA setting.

jokes.text: How ingenious. The forks are made out of Lego.
jokes.setup: Homer admiring IKEA design philosophy
jokes.punchline: His appreciation for the impractical Lego fork design
jokes.explanation: Works as Homer moment but relatively simple. His genuine admiration for terrible design ideas fits his character.

jokes.text: Hey, Apu. Manjula. You guys are still married?
jokes.setup: Homer greeting the couple casually
jokes.punchline: His assumption that marriages commonly dissolve quickly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's casual tactlessness and cynical view of marriage stability. Economical and believable.

jokes.text: Pay up, Marge.
jokes.setup: Learning Apu and Manjula are still married
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had a bet with Marge about their marriage lasting
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - three words reveal an entire backstory about Homer betting against the marriage. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Maggie loves baby talk. That was Hindi.
jokes.setup: Homer assumes Manjula was doing baby talk
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was speaking her native language
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that also addresses cultural assumptions. Well-structured reveal with good character integration for Manjula.

jokes.text: On the other hand, monkey see, monkey do.
jokes.setup: Apu considering having children carefully
jokes.punchline: Homer's oversimplified approach to major life decisions
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - reducing complex decision to simple animal behavior. Fits his philosophy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: You can teach 'em to hate the things you hate. And they practically raise themselves, what with the Internet and all.
jokes.setup: Homer selling Apu on having kids
jokes.punchline: His terrible parenting advice focusing on hatred and neglect
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Homer. His sincerely terrible parenting philosophy delivered as helpful advice. Good structure with escalating bad ideas.

jokes.text: I've noticed this country is dangerously underpopulated.
jokes.setup: Apu deciding it's time to have children
jokes.punchline: His completely backwards assessment of US population
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation. Apu's sincere but completely wrong demographic analysis works as both character moment and social commentary.

jokes.text: Who will float my corpse down the Ganges?
jokes.setup: Apu explaining why he wants children
jokes.punchline: His morbid but culturally specific motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that's both culturally specific and universally funny. Perfect Apu characterization with memorable, quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: Oh, Apu, take me now. Oh, Calcutta!
jokes.setup: Manjula being turned on by Apu's romantic words
jokes.punchline: Using 'Oh, Calcutta!' as sexual exclamation (play on Broadway show)
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that works as both geography and Broadway show. Good character moment for Manjula with economical delivery.

jokes.text: By chilling my loins, I increase the chances of impregnating my wife.
jokes.setup: Moe asking why Apu is in the ice cream cooler
jokes.punchline: Apu's clinical, oversharing explanation of fertility strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. Apu's matter-of-fact delivery of TMI medical information creates uncomfortable humor. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Whoa-ho! Too much information! Thanks for the mental picture! Why don't you tell us what you really think?
jokes.setup: Apu's oversharing about fertility methods
jokes.punchline: Moe's escalating discomfort delivered in hackneyed TV phrases
jokes.explanation: Weak follow-up that relies on clichéd responses. The 'hackneyed quips' line that follows suggests this might be intentionally bad, but still feels lazy.

jokes.text: Would you stop spouting those hackneyed quips? Could you be any more- Hello!
jokes.setup: Apu criticizing Moe's clichéd responses
jokes.punchline: Cuts himself off mid-cliché when customer enters
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke that calls out clichéd sitcom responses, then catches Apu doing the same thing. Self-aware comedy with good timing.

jokes.text: Um, how 'bout one not touching your ass?
jokes.setup: Barney asking for ice cream while Apu sits in freezer
jokes.punchline: His reasonable but crude request for hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor. Barney's practical concern about food safety in this absurd situation feels authentic to character.

jokes.text: Whoa. Manjula has begun to ovulate.
jokes.setup: Apu's pager goes off
jokes.punchline: His clinical announcement of wife's biological process
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Apu's TMI fertility obsession. His matter-of-fact delivery of intimate information maintains character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, lemon.
jokes.setup: Negative pregnancy test result
jokes.punchline: Apu's mild, food-related expletive for major disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character moment. Using food item as mild curse fits his background and creates understated reaction to major disappointment.

jokes.text: All that sex for nothing.
jokes.setup: Failed pregnancy attempt
jokes.punchline: Apu treating sex as purely utilitarian chore
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Apu's practical approach to reproduction. Understated but effective.

jokes.text: But the cans seal in the flavor.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer open exploding cans
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided food safety logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food logic. His commitment to preserving flavor over safety shows his priorities and ignorance.

jokes.text: Decided? That's classic. 'Decided.'
jokes.setup: Apu asking about deciding to have children
jokes.punchline: Homer finding the concept of planning pregnancy hilarious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization. His incredulity at family planning reveals his approach to major life decisions. Good repeated word emphasis.

jokes.text: Oh! Homer, we have tried everything- oysters, gravity boots, Sanjay's bed, every possible position.
jokes.setup: Manjula explaining their fertility efforts
jokes.punchline: List escalates from normal to weird to TMI
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal fertility aids to bizarre specifics. The oversharing fits established character patterns.

jokes.text: Really? On top and underneath?
jokes.setup: Manjula mentioning trying every position
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent but inappropriate clarifying question
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike curiosity about adult topics creates awkward humor. His innocent delivery makes it funnier than crude.

jokes.text: Uh, I'm willing to play the high school jock... but did you have to cut the roof off my car?
jokes.setup: Homer's elaborate roleplay scenario for conception
jokes.punchline: Apu's practical concern about property damage
jokes.explanation: Good setup for absurd situation. Apu's willingness to roleplay but concern for car shows his priorities and desperation.

jokes.text: Hup-hup-hup-hup. That's an Apu question. You're Greg.
jokes.setup: Apu breaking character with practical question
jokes.punchline: Homer's commitment to maintaining roleplay immersion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer directing moment. His serious commitment to absurd roleplay scenario shows his creative investment in helping Apu.

jokes.text: I- I thought I was going to Ivy League State. My mistake. Stay in the moment.
jokes.setup: Homer's confusing narrative about Vietnam and college
jokes.punchline: Quick correction while maintaining roleplay commitment
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's improvisation skills and Apu's literal-minded approach to the scenario. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: Baby. Baby. Pirate?
jokes.setup: Couple excited about pregnancy test result
jokes.punchline: Confusion about what they're seeing on the test
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy with three words creating confusion and visual comedy. The randomness of 'pirate' on pregnancy test is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: No, no, wait a minute! Pirates are wild! We are pregnant!
jokes.setup: Confusion about pregnancy test showing pirate
jokes.punchline: Manjula's logical connection between pirates and pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. Manjula's earnest reasoning that pirates=wild=pregnant creates perfect non-sequitur comedy with confident delivery.

jokes.text: I'll say. I learned the true meaning of Columbus Day.
jokes.setup: Agreeing the last nine months were eventful
jokes.punchline: His random, meaningless personal revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer non-sequitur. His earnest delivery of meaningless personal growth about obscure holiday fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: I became the most popular girl in school... but blew it by being conceited. And then I learned the true meaning of winter.
jokes.setup: Lisa recounting her eventful nine months
jokes.punchline: Escalation from realistic to absurd personal revelations
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's random revelations while maintaining Lisa's character. The escalation from plausible to absurd works well.

jokes.text: You are beautiful and silky and manageable. You're reading that off a conditioner bottle.
jokes.setup: Apu giving romantic compliment to pregnant wife
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he's literally reading product description
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection comedy. Sets up romantic moment then reveals desperate improvisation. Good character moment for both.

jokes.text: Gil's moving up to the big leagues, boy. Oh, my back.
jokes.setup: Gil excited about cleanup opportunity
jokes.punchline: Immediately injures himself on simple task
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gil characterization - optimism immediately followed by failure. Quick but effective character moment.

jokes.text: We had quite a discussion about the funniest way to reveal them to you.
jokes.setup: Revealing octuplets to shocked father
jokes.punchline: Medical staff treating family crisis as comedy writing exercise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy about comedy itself. The idea that doctors would workshop the reveal for maximum humor is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: It rolls off your tongue and into your heart- 'octuplets.'
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining the medical term
jokes.punchline: Treating medical terminology like poetry
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that treats clinical term romantically. Shows doctor's inappropriate enthusiasm for the situation.

jokes.text: Yeah, that. It looked just like that.
jokes.setup: Homer describing Apu's reaction to seeing babies
jokes.punchline: Apu is making the same shocked face again
jokes.explanation: Visual callback joke - Apu still in shock, Homer's observational accuracy about facial expressions

jokes.text: Well, that would only account for quintuplets. Did anyone else slip this woman fertility drugs?
jokes.setup: Apu confessing to drugging Manjula's breakfast
jokes.punchline: His mathematical approach to multiple drug dosing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy. Apu's logical problem-solving approach to explain extra babies is both mathematical and absurd. Great setup for Homer's reveal.

jokes.text: Mine tasted like strawberry. Mmm. 'Ovulicious.'
jokes.setup: Homer admitting to also drugging Manjula
jokes.punchline: His appreciation for the flavor and made-up portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment. His focus on flavor over consequences plus invented word 'Ovulicious' shows his priorities and creativity. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Would you say you and your babies have a 'love eight' relationship? No, say it! We need a sound bite.
jokes.setup: News reporter interviewing new parents
jokes.punchline: Forcing terrible pun and admitting manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good media satire showing reporter's artificial soundbite creation. Kent's character-appropriate pushiness makes the cringe effective.

jokes.text: How would you respond to people who say this kind of multiple birth... is more suited to, say, a possum than a human being? Who would say such a thing? Well, pundits, wags. I'm not the one on trial here.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking inflammatory question
jokes.punchline: Kent's defensive response to reasonable pushback
jokes.explanation: Good media critique showing how reporters hide behind 'some say' framing. Kent's defensive response shows his awareness of manipulation.

jokes.text: Well, pundits, wags. I'm not the one on trial here.
jokes.setup: Asked who would say such cruel things
jokes.punchline: Blames unnamed sources while deflecting responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent Brockman - asks inflammatory questions then acts defensive when called out

jokes.text: Free baby cola! Apu hits the jackpot and I'm stuck with these useless one-tuplets!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Apu's corporate sponsorship
jokes.punchline: Jealousy expressed through invented word 'one-tuplets'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer wordplay. 'One-tuplets' is both linguistically clever and reveals his jealousy and commodification of children. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Gee, sorry for being born. I've been waiting so long to hear that.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about his 'useless' children
jokes.punchline: Homer's satisfaction with child's self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly calibrated Homer parenting moment. His gleeful response to Bart's existential despair is both horrible and funny.

jokes.text: They only gave us three crates. They can rot in hell.
jokes.setup: Apu praising generous companies
jokes.punchline: Condemns Q-tip company to eternal damnation for insufficient free products
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - goes from grateful to vindictive over corporate generosity differences, proportionally insane response

jokes.text: Some say eight babies is a blessing... but they don't know the joy of nine. Would you say you're on 'cloud nine'?
jokes.setup: Shelbyville having nine babies vs Springfield's eight
jokes.punchline: Forced 'cloud nine' pun that actually works contextually
jokes.explanation: Works as escalation of media competition and callback to forced puns, though less sharp than earlier examples.

jokes.text: Nine babies? That's barbaric! Nonuplets? Now that's something you don't see every day.
jokes.setup: Homer's moral outrage at nine babies
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to fascination with the terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction moment. His instant shift from moral judgment to linguistic fascination shows his scattered priorities.

jokes.text: Oh, I just had the most beautiful dream where I died.
jokes.setup: Apu being woken up for work after no sleep
jokes.punchline: Finding death preferable to current situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Apu's desperation. His sincere appreciation for death as escape from parenting is both funny and relatable.

jokes.text: How do you get cradle rash when you sleep in a suitcase?
jokes.setup: Apu complaining about babies' problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals their terrible sleeping arrangements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy revealing increasing desperation. The casual mention of suitcase sleeping shows how bad things have gotten.

jokes.text: I can't believe you don't shut up.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to offer cheerful parenting platitudes
jokes.punchline: Apu's exhausted, grammar-challenged frustration
jokes.explanation: Shows Apu's exhaustion affecting his usually precise English. The broken grammar reveals how tired he is while maintaining character.

jokes.text: Although he thinks one or two might just be going along with the crowd.
jokes.setup: Discussing babies having colic
jokes.punchline: Apu suspects babies of peer pressure conformity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd parenting observation - attributing social dynamics to infant medical conditions

jokes.text: Eight kids? Mmm. I'm sterile, right, baby doll? Yes, dear. From the nuclear plant. Beautiful.
jokes.setup: Barney hearing about eight babies
jokes.punchline: His relief about nuclear-induced sterility
jokes.explanation: Dark environmental comedy. Barney's gratitude for radiation poisoning as birth control is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Welcome to my nightmare.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge arriving to help with babies
jokes.punchline: Apu's dramatic but understated greeting
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character moment. Apu's formal delivery of Alice Cooper reference shows his desperation while maintaining dignity.

jokes.text: Oh, hallelujah. Our problems are solved. We have banana bread.
jokes.setup: Marge offering banana bread as help
jokes.punchline: Apu's sarcastic overreaction to inadequate help
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm showing Apu's frustration with inadequate help. The religious exclamation adds emphasis to his desperation.

jokes.text: Banana bread! What the hell were you thinking? Banana bread.
jokes.setup: Apu's mounting frustration
jokes.punchline: His fixation on banana bread as symbol of inadequate help
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Apu's breakdown. The repetition of 'banana bread' shows his spiraling mental state.

jokes.text: Banana bread! What the hell were you thinking? Banana bread.
jokes.setup: Continuing to process Marge's gift choice
jokes.punchline: Apu's delayed outrage at the specific inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - Apu can't let go of how wrong the gesture was

jokes.text: As a token of forgiveness, please take this baby.
jokes.setup: Apu apologizing for his rudeness
jokes.punchline: Offers to give away one of his children as apology gift
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating children like party favors, reveals desperation while maintaining social politeness

jokes.text: Who cares? There's only one of him.
jokes.setup: Apu warns about trusting mysterious stranger
jokes.punchline: Manjula's logic that single person is manageable compared to eight babies
jokes.explanation: Good parental desperation logic - stranger danger less concerning than quantity management

jokes.text: What? Everybody loves my zoo. You don't love my zoo? I dare you to look at a kangaroo and not laugh. I dare you.
jokes.setup: Apu rejecting zoo proposal
jokes.punchline: Zookeeper's defensive kangaroo challenge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for the zookeeper. His defensive pride in kangaroo entertainment value feels authentic to his sleazy character.

jokes.text: If your mother was a vampire and your father was a Frankenstein... how come you are a werewolf? Huh. I never thought of that. Doesn't make sense, does it?
jokes.setup: Homer questioning Munsters family genetics
jokes.punchline: Child actor realizing the plot hole in his own show
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy pointing out actual Munsters plot inconsistency. Homer's logical question breaks the fourth wall effectively.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm a notary. So I got that goin' too.
jokes.setup: Kidkill producing legal documents quickly
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of additional professional credential
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Kidkill's sleazy preparedness. The casual delivery makes it funnier than dramatic reveal.

jokes.text: If you're not in a diaper, get off my stage.
jokes.setup: Preparing for baby show
jokes.punchline: Stage direction based on infant clothing requirements
jokes.explanation: Good absurd showbiz direction adapted for baby performers

jokes.text: Welcome to Octopia!
jokes.setup: Introducing the octuplets show
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'octuplets' and 'utopia'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that captures the exploitative nature of the show. Good economy and fits the sleazy showman character.

jokes.text: Say hello to the baddest baby in the whole damn town-Animal.
jokes.setup: Introducing baby performer
jokes.punchline: Treating infant as tough rock star
jokes.explanation: Good absurd juxtaposition of baby innocence with rock star introduction. Shows the exploitation clearly.

jokes.text: How can he rebel? He doesn't even know where he is.
jokes.setup: Baby performing as rebellious rocker
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical observation about infant cognition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - applying maternal logic to absurd performance situation. Her practical nature cuts through the showbiz nonsense.

jokes.text: And now the stand-up who can't even sit up... give it up for Punch Line!
jokes.setup: Introducing another baby performer
jokes.punchline: Puns on infant developmental stage with comedy terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever pun combining infant development with comedy performance

jokes.text: Work with Dazzle. She's a lox out there.
jokes.setup: Giving performance notes to babies
jokes.punchline: Treats infant like failing professional performer
jokes.explanation: Absurd show business criticism applied to baby performance

jokes.text: Paid me off? What, are you crazy? Mmm. Mmm.
jokes.setup: Accused of being bribed by zookeeper
jokes.punchline: Denies corruption while actively eating bribe food
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum obliviousness - obvious corruption while claiming innocence

jokes.text: Well, they might give Dazzle back. The buzz is she's got one more show to turn it around.
jokes.setup: Asked if they'll ever see their children again
jokes.punchline: Treats baby like failing TV show with ratings concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - applies entertainment industry standards to infant performance

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, a lot of people are doing that these days.
jokes.setup: Apu saying they'll take law into their own hands
jokes.punchline: Wiggum treats vigilantism as casual trend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum casual acceptance of law breakdown as social fad

jokes.text: Show's over! Show's over! Next show at 8:00 and 10:00.
jokes.setup: Chaos with cobras attacking everyone
jokes.punchline: Still advertising next shows despite disaster
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Kidkill's commercial priorities even during crisis. The automatic showtime announcement amid chaos works well.

jokes.text: Stupid mongoose! Get the snake! The snake! Do I look like a snake?
jokes.setup: Mongoose released to fight cobras
jokes.punchline: Mongoose attacks Eddie Munster instead of snakes
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation with former child star as victim

tmdb_overview: Due to overuse of fertility medication, Apu and wife Manjula give birth to octuplets. Desperate for money, Apu loans the babies to an unscrupulous carny promoter, then needs Homer's help to get them back.
